articles.title: BUSYWORK IS THE CREATIVITY KILLER 😵💫
articles.summary: Chasing campaign updates, following up with stakeholders, looking for assets—that's time _not_ spent on creativity or strategy.

articles.title: Try Asana for free — no credit card required
LABELLING SOCIAL MEDIA ‘ADDICTIVE' IS TOO SIMPLISTIC
articles.summary: A new study challenges the idea that social media is inherently addictive for teens. It shows that both heavy use and complete abstinence can harm well-being. Researchers found a “sweet spot” where moderate use supports happiness, life satisfaction, and school engagement, with patterns varying by age and gender. Girls face higher risks from heavy use, while older boys may struggle if disconnected. Experts suggest balanced digital engagement with age-appropriate limits is more effective than blanket bans.

articles.title: THE RISE OF IN-CAR ADS
articles.summary: Automakers are exploring infotainment screens as a major source of ad revenue. They're integrating subtle promotions like nearby restaurants or gas stations into connected car systems while phasing out phone-mirroring interfaces like CarPlay and Android Auto. Companies like 4screen partner with multiple brands to deliver nonintrusive, utility-focused ads. Ford has patented systems to target ads via cameras or audio. With 97% of new cars connected and potential e-commerce revenue of $100-120 per car by 2030, the in-car ad market could exceed $50B globally.

articles.title: THE 3-EMAIL CART & CHECKOUT ABANDONMENT FLOWS
articles.summary: Cart abandoners add items to their cart but never start checkout. They're usually browsing or comparing products. Checkout abandoners enter their email or shipping info and were ready to buy but got interrupted. Both flows convert by targeting users with intent, removing friction, and nudging completion. The 3-email framework sends reminders at 1 hour, 24 hours, and 48-72 hours. It progresses from a gentle nudge, to objection handling, and a final push with urgency.

articles.title: 5 WAYS TO REFRAME YOUR PRICING AND PACKAGING
articles.summary: This piece shares how SaaS teams can reframe pricing to drive growth and retention. Klaviyo redesigned its pricing page to spotlight its free plan. Lindy repositioned Enterprise around outcomes by centering its AI Assistant. Eden AI reframed Professional Services as part of an Advanced AI Platform to support recurring revenue. DeepBrain made annual plans the default to anchor larger commitments. Hugging Face elevated Data Storage as a core add-on to increase stickiness and recurring revenue.

articles.title: DON'T BLAME YOUR AI TOOLS FOR CONTENT PERFORMANCE ISSUES. FIX THE CONTEXT INSTEAD.
articles.summary: Disappointed by AI's marginal impact on content performance? The problem isn't the model—it's the missing context.

articles.title: FROM 2025 TO 2026: SIGNALS, SHIFTS, AND WHAT TO PREPARE FOR (WEBINAR)
articles.summary: This Omnisend webinar breaks down what marketers should prepare for in 2026. The speakers use a full year of performance data to review key 2025 metrics. They cover customer behavior and channel trends. They also share evidence-based predictions on what is changing versus what is overhyped, with a focus on email and e-commerce marketing for the year ahead. It will take place today at 8 AM PT/11 AM ET.

articles.title: CONTENT SERIES BUILDER
articles.summary: SEO Workflows' new tool automates topical authority. Input the core themes you want to build content around. The tool identifies prioritized parent topics within the themes, key target personas with pain points, and subtopic ideas to create a content series.

articles.title: GSC DATA IS 75% INCOMPLETE
articles.summary: GSC is unreliable as a single source because Google filters about 75% of impressions and 38% of clicks for privacy. An analysis of 10 B2B SaaS sites with ~450M impressions found filtering from 59.3% to 93.6% for impressions and up to 88.5% for clicks, leaving large gaps in query data. AI Overviews surged in 2025 and correlate with a 56.6% click drop since March, while impressions fluctuated due to scraper activity. Bots also inflate impressions by 0.2% to 6.5%. Relying only on GSC can mislead teams, so use multiple data sources and sampling checks.

articles.title: FROM CLAWDBOT TO MOLTBOT TO OPENCLAW: THE AI AGENT GENERATING BUZZ AND FEAR
articles.summary: OpenClaw, an open-source AI agent launched weeks ago by Peter Steinberger, has surged to over 145,000 GitHub stars as interest grows in agents that can autonomously act across operating systems. The tool runs directly on user devices and connects to models like Claude or ChatGPT. It can manage emails, calendars, files, shopping, and web tasks using persistent memory. Security firms, including Palo Alto Networks and Cisco, have warned that its deep system access creates serious risks around data leakage, malicious commands, and enterprise safety.
